item.title: Brain-Inspired Visual Sensing–perception for Open-World Environments
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 15 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00095-3

A brain-inspired two-stage sensing–perception framework couples bottom-up visual priors with top-down adaptive modulation, enabling robust open-world vision under extreme real-world degradations.

item.title: A Spike–language Dual Framework Bridges Fast Perception and Deep Reasoning in Artificial Tactile Somatosensory Systems
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 15 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00108-1

Spike–language dual encoding integrates tactile sensors with spiking neural networks and large language models to enhance robotic perception and cognition in object classification and semantic reasoning, bridging gaps in tactile processing.

item.title: Temporal Programmable In-Sensor System for Multi-Speed Motion Perception
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00101-8

A temporally programmable in-sensor system using ferroelectric–charge trapping transistors enables multi-speed motion perception with wide tunable dynamics, low crosstalk and scalable arrays for efficient real-time analysis in dynamic environments.

item.title: Clarifying the Landscape of Mechanical Sensors from Stress to Strain
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00090-8

Stress and strain sensors underpin mechanical sensing across electronics, robotics and bioengineering, yet their distinct physical meanings and response pathways are often confused. This Review establishes a unifying, mechanics-based framework that clarifies stress- versus strain-driven sensing, guiding rational device design, performance evaluation and application-specific sensor selection.

item.title: Ten Questions on the Future of Sensing
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00093-5

As sensing technologies become increasingly multi- and inter-disciplinary, their role is rapidly evolving beyond signal detection. In this Viewpoint, five experts discuss emerging sensing paradigms, ethical and societal challenges, and the system-level innovations needed to translate laboratory breakthroughs into robust, trustworthy technologies for real-world deployment.

item.title: High-Fidelity Dispersive Spin Sensing in a Tunable Unit Cell of Silicon MOS Quantum Dots
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00084-6

A compact, industrially fabricated single electron box sensor enables state-of-the-art high-fidelity dispersive spin readout within a tunable silicon MOS quantum dot unit cell.

item.title: A Self-Powered Capsule for Drinking Water Safety
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00110-7

A self-powered capsule for drinking water safety

item.title: High-Resolution Tracking of Global Tree Cover Loss Drivers
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00113-4

High-resolution tracking of global tree cover loss drivers

item.title: Shaping the AI Systems that Speak for Science
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00109-0

Researchers are increasingly discovering and consuming scientific knowledge through artificial intelligence (AI) platforms built by technology companies. Here we discuss what publishers and editors can do to co-create use-cases and governance of the AI systems that mediate science.

item.title: Improved Perception for Machine Vision Under Complex Illumination
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 01 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00104-5

Optical interference from lasers and intense lighting can confuse machine vision. Now, a tunable band-stop photodetector has been designed that uses machine learning to suppress disruptive spectral components at the sensor, enabling more reliable perception in challenging illumination.

item.title: Tunable Band-Stop Photodetection with Machine Learning-Enabled Broadband Spectral Adaptation
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 01 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00098-0

A machine learning-guided tunable band-stop photodetector enables adaptive suppression of unpredictable light interference for robust broadband machine vision.

item.title: AI Clones in Conversation at Academic Conferences
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 26 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00089-1

Artificial intelligence (AI) clones could serve as a new interface for scientific communication between publishers and research communities. At a Nature conference in Seoul, multimodal AI clones — including one of the Chief Editor of Nature Sensors — engaged researchers in real-time dialogue about publishing and careers, in three different languages.

item.title: Non-Contact Biomechanical Profiling of 3D Tissues
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 26 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00096-2

A wireless biomechanical sensing platform enables non-contact, scalable, cost-effective profiling of three-dimensional (3D) cardiac organoids and engineered heart tissues.

item.title: Wireless and Contactless Biomechanic Well Plate for Monitoring Cardiac Organoid and 3D-Tissue Contraction
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 26 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00087-3

A wireless and contactless biomechanical well plate leverages ultrasensitive silicon cantilevers to enable high-throughput, real-time quantification of contractile forces in cardiac organoids and engineered heart tissues.

item.title: Integrated Thermal Sensing of Vascular Morphology for Haemodynamic Monitoring
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00082-8

A wearable thermal sensing approach enables non-invasive inference of vascular depth and diameter from skin thermal transients, improving the accuracy of haemodynamic modelling.

item.title: In-Sensor Spectral Decoding for Efficient Spectroscopic Recognition
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00085-5

In-sensor spectral decoding enables direct spectral reconstruction and application-level recognition without off-chip computation, delivering efficient spectroscopic sensing.

item.title: Multiparametric Molecular Imaging of the Brain
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 09 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00088-2

Multiparametric molecular imaging of the brain
